register.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

footer.disclaimer: Research portfolio project. Educational use only - not a medical device.

analysis.title: New analysis
analysis.intro: Upload a single histopathology patch to run it through the research model.
analysis.disclaimer: This tool is an educational and research project, not a medical device. It does not provide a diagnosis and must not be used for clinical decisions.
analysis.submitError: Something went wrong while analyzing this image. Please try again.

upload.dropTitle: Drag and drop an image, or click to choose a file
upload.modelUnavailable: The analysis model is currently unavailable. Please try again shortly.
upload.requirements: Images must be exactly {{width}} × {{height}} pixels, PNG or JPEG, up to {{maxSize}}.

errors.empty: This file is empty. Choose a different image.
errors.unsupportedType: Only PNG and JPEG images are supported.
errors.tooLarge: This file is larger than the {{maxSize}} limit. Choose a smaller image.
errors.corrupt: This file could not be read as an image. It may be corrupted - try a different file.
errors.dimensionsMismatch: This image is {{actualWidth}} × {{actualHeight}} px, but the model requires exactly {{expectedWidth}} × {{expectedHeight}} px. Crop or resize the image and try again.

confirm.title: Ready to analyze
confirm.processingNotice: The image is processed in memory for this analysis only and is not stored. Results require expert interpretation and are not a diagnosis.

syntheticNotice.synthetic: This is a synthetic test model used for demonstration. Its output has no medical meaning whatsoever.
syntheticNotice.unknown: This result may have been produced by a synthetic test model with no medical meaning. The currently active model could not be confirmed to match.

result.genericError: We could not load this analysis. Please try again.
result.interpretationGuidance: The model assigned the highest probability to this category. This result requires expert interpretation and is not a medical diagnosis.
result.limitationNotice: This tool does not provide a medical diagnosis. It is a research demonstration and may be wrong, especially on images unlike its training data.

probabilities.title: Class probabilities
probabilities.subtitle: All categories the model considered, with their probability.
probabilities.unavailable: Probabilities are not available for this result.

image.title: Original image
image.notStored: The original image is not stored and can only be shown immediately after analysis.

gradcam.title: Visual explanation (Grad-CAM)
gradcam.explanation: Grad-CAM highlights regions that influenced the model's output. It does not prove why the model decided what it did, is not a lesion boundary, a segmentation result, or a clinical annotation.
gradcam.overlayAlt: Grad-CAM highlighted regions overlaid on the image
gradcam.disclaimer: Grad-CAM highlights regions associated with the model output. It is not a biological explanation and must not be used as a diagnosis.

unavailable.not_requested: A visual explanation was not requested for this analysis.
unavailable.failed: Generating a visual explanation failed for this analysis. The prediction result above is still valid.
unavailable.disabled: Visual explanations are currently disabled for this model.
unavailable.never_persisted: Visual explanations are only available immediately after running an analysis and are not stored afterwards.

history.title: Analysis history
history.intro: Your past analyses. Images and visual explanations are not stored - only the result.
history.error: We could not load your analysis history. Please try again.
history.empty: You haven't analyzed any images yet.

howItWorks.title: From upload to a transparent result
howItWorks.intro: The research demo follows three steps, every time.

sections.heading: 1. Upload a patch
sections.body: You upload a single PNG or JPEG histopathology patch at the exact size the active model expects. The image is processed in memory and is never stored.

sections.heading: 2. Model inference
sections.body: A binary classifier scores the patch and returns a decision - negative, positive, or review required - along with calibrated probabilities for both classes.

sections.heading: 3. Grad-CAM explanation
sections.body: An optional Grad-CAM overlay highlights the regions that most influenced the model's output. It explains the model, not the underlying biology, and is not a diagnosis.

technology.title: What this project is built with
technology.intro: A small, transparent stack chosen for clarity over scale.

sections.heading: Backend
sections.body: A FastAPI service backed by PostgreSQL, with a PyTorch model serving layer that enforces upload validation, concurrency limits, and safe error handling.

sections.heading: Frontend
sections.body: A React and TypeScript single-page app with a typed client generated directly from the backend's OpenAPI schema, so the UI can't silently drift from the real API.

model.title: What the active model actually is
model.intro: Plain facts about the model behind this demo - no marketing claims.

sections.heading: A synthetic baseline, not a clinical model
sections.body: The model currently active in this demo is a small baseline CNN trained on synthetic data for engineering purposes. Its predictions have no medical meaning whatsoever.

sections.heading: Binary classification with calibrated probabilities
sections.body: It outputs a calibrated probability for one of two classes, plus an optional Grad-CAM visual explanation. It has not been evaluated against any real clinical dataset.

privacy.title: What happens to an uploaded image
privacy.intro: Designed so there is as little sensitive data to protect as possible.

sections.heading: Images are never stored
sections.body: An uploaded image is decoded, validated, and processed in memory for a single analysis, then discarded. The raw file is never written to disk or to the database.

sections.heading: Only the result is kept
sections.body: Your account stores the prediction result (decision, probabilities, timestamps) so you can revisit it later - never the image itself or the Grad-CAM overlay.

limitations.title: What this demo cannot do
limitations.intro: Read this before drawing any conclusion from a result.

sections.heading: Not validated for clinical use
sections.body: This is a research and educational portfolio project. It must never be used to diagnose, treat, or make decisions about real patients.

sections.heading: One small, synthetic model
sections.body: Only one binary classifier, trained on synthetic data, is deployed. It does not support whole-slide images, multiple cancer types, or any real diagnostic workflow.

accessibility.title: Built to be usable by more people
accessibility.intro: Accessibility preferences are available throughout the signed-in app.

sections.heading: Adjustable text size, contrast and motion
sections.body: Signed-in users can increase text size, switch to a higher-contrast theme, and reduce motion from the Accessibility page - the preference applies across the whole app.

sections.heading: Keyboard and screen-reader support
sections.body: Interactive elements are keyboard-operable with visible focus states, and dynamic content (validation messages, analysis progress) is announced to screen readers.

sections.heading: A portfolio demo, not a production service
sections.body: This deployment has no uptime guarantee or service-level agreement. It may be restarted, reset, or unavailable without notice.
